Title: The Iris & The Rose
Body (HTML):
The Iris (fleur de lis) and the Rose (Tudor Rose). The fleur-de-lis is a historical symbol of the French Monarchy. It appeared in ancient civilizations, was consolidated in French heraldry, and is associated with nobility, religion, and popular culture. The Tudor rose (sometimes called the Union rose) is the traditional floral heraldic emblem of England and takes its name and origins from the House of Tudor, which united the house of York to the house of Lancaster. This pattern unites the British to the French in an ancient tapestry pattern.

Available as Professional-grade removable wallpaper. Easy to install and remove without damage.
